name: Upside-Down Orange Biscuits W/Variation
recipeInstructions:
------------
Combine flour, baking powder and salt. Cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in milk just until moistened. Turn onto a lightly floured surface; knead gently for 10 to 12 times. Roll into a rectangle measuring 15x12 inches. Spread with butter.
Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over butter. Roll up jelly-roll style, starting from the short end. Cut into 12 equal slices. Place cut side down in a greased 9-inch round baking pan.
Combine topping ingredients; pour over biscuits. Bake at 450°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool in pan for 5 minutes before serving warm.
Variation:
- Use 4 tbsp butter instead of 3 tbsp shortening in the biscuit dough. Knead gently for 8 to 10 times. Roll into a rectangle measuring 11x8 inches.
- Brush with 1 tbsp melted butter.
- Combine sugar and cinnamon; spread over butter.
- Prepare as above, baking in an 8-inch square pan brushed with 1 tbsp melted butter. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes.
- Spread topping over warm biscuits and serve immediately.
